title: Timeout Layers, Deadlines, Cancellation, Cleanup, and Budget Allocation · Automation and Data Client Systems · Code Recall
summary: A compliance analyst reconstructing a partial ingestion handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for timeout layers, deadlines, cancellation, cleanup, and budget allocation, producing end-to-end request budget and cancellation model. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers end-to-end request budget and cancellation model after a delayed changed cas…
objectives: Separate name resolution, connect, TLS, response-header, body-read, idle, and whole-operation timeout controls. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Propagate caller cancellation and deadlines through request creation, helpers, parsing, retries, and downstream work. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Allocate an end-to-end latency budget across attempts and phases while preserving time for cleanup and fallback. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Timeout Classification
steps.instruction:
review case — compliance handoff automation has 900 ms end-to-end for DNS, connect, TLS, headers, streamed body, parse, and cleanup. A caller cancels during body read; allocate phase budgets, propagate the reason, stop owned work, and preserve unknown remote effect. Competency probe: Report which phase and policy ended an operation without exposing secrets or claiming the remote effect is known. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Inspect the Timeout Classification trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
